mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Persistent inflation above 3.1% (CPIAUCSL) triggers central bank rate hikes, increasing discount rates and reducing equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of instances where CPIAUCSL exceeds 310 despite the signal.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in the federal funds rate and inflation (FEDFUNDS > 5% and CPIAUCSL > 310) signal a tightening cycle with rising risk-free rates, compressing equity multiples and generating negative S&P 500 returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: SPX returns are positive in > 50% of periods where both FEDFUNDS > 5% and CPIAUCSL > 310.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp rise in unemployment (UNRATE > 0.3pp in 1 month) signals deteriorating labor market conditions, reducing corporate earnings visibility and generating negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX returns are positive in > 60% of instances where UNRATE rises > 0.3pp in 1 month.

mechanism_narrative: Strong GDP growth (GDPC1 > 2% annualized) indicates robust economic expansion, boosting corporate profits and generating positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX returns are negative in > 50% of periods where GDPC1 exceeds 2% annualized.

mechanism_narrative: Weak housing starts (HOUST < 1,350k) signal reduced consumer confidence and lower construction activity, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of instances where HOUST falls below 1,350k.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in payrolls (PAYEMS > 150k) and declining unemployment (UNRATE < 4.5%) signals a strong labor market, boosting consumer spending and generating positive S&P 500 returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: SPX returns are negative in > 50% of periods where PAYEMS exceeds 150k and UNRATE falls below 4.5%.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in industrial production (INDPRO < 102.5) indicates weakening manufacturing activity, reducing corporate earnings and generating negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5d returns in > 60% of instances where INDPRO falls below 102.5.
